name: Firing Range Loop
name_original: Cabezo de las Cortinillas y Castildetierra – circular por el Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales
description: An easy 10.8-mile mountain biking route in Bardenas Reales, featuring the iconic Castildetierra and panoramic views from Cabezo de las Corti
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Arguedas are primarily found within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This semi-desert environment is characterized by its unique "lunar landscape," featuring dramatic rock formations, vast plateaus, and deep gorges. The terrain offers a diverse setting for mountain biking, ranging from flat, open tracks to more challenging routes through eroded badlands. This region provides a distinctive backdrop for outdoor activities, with its striking geological features. Best mountain bike trails around Arguedas The most popular mountain bike trail is Firing Range Loop, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route features the iconic Castildetierra formation within the Bardenas Reales. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tour of Bardenas Reales by mountain bike, a difficult 50.2 miles (80.7 km) path. This extensive route traverses the semi-desert landscape, offering views of its unique geological formations. Local mountain bikers also love the Bardenas Reales Natural Park – Castildetierra Hill loop from Arguedas, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail leading through the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Arguedas is defined by its semi-desert environment, dramatic rock formations, and vast plateaus. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to demanding long-distance routes. The routes in Arguedas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arguedas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Arguedas?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails around Arguedas, primarily located within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from easy loops to challenging long-distance rides across the semi-desert landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking near Arguedas?
A: The terrain around Arguedas is largely defined by the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, a unique semi-desert environment. You'll encounter a 'lunar landscape' with dramatic rock formations, vast plateaus, and deep gorges. Trails range from flat, open tracks to more technical routes through eroded badlands, offering a distinctive backdrop for mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Arguedas?
A: Yes, Arguedas offers several easy mountain bike trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988492546/firing-range-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Firing Range Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 17.4 km route that takes about 1 hour 23 minutes to complete and features the iconic Castildetierra formation.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in the Bardenas Reales?
A: The Bardenas Reales Natural Park is rich with unique landmarks. You can encounter the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, a striking earthen castle-like formation. Other notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Cortinillas&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself, which offers a stunning semi-desert landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the Bardenas Reales are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14941675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park – Castildetierra Hill loop from Arguedas&lt;/a&gt;, a 46.2 km trail that typically takes around 3 hours 35 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Arguedas?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Arguedas and the Bardenas Reales is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold with occasional rain.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in the Bardenas Reales Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on designated trails within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations before your visit, as rules can change, especially concerning protected areas or specific events.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Bardenas Reales?
A: While the Bardenas Reales Natural Park is a natural area, regulations regarding dogs can vary. It's best to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the arid environment and potential for challenging terrain. Always check the latest park guidelines for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike options around Arguedas?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly mountain bike options, particularly on the easier, flatter tracks within the Bardenas Reales. These routes allow families to enjoy the unique landscape together. The town of Arguedas itself also offers attractions like Sendaviva Natural Park, which can complement a family outdoor trip.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Arguedas?
A: The mountain bike trails around Arguedas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Riders frequently praise the unique 'lunar landscape' of the Bardenas Reales, the dramatic rock formations, and the diverse range of trails suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: Arguedas serves as a primary hub for visitors to the Bardenas Reales and offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options within the town. For those looking for a scenic stay, the area around Virgen del Yugo, located on a high point outside the urban center, features a hermitage, a Bardena viewpoint, and a hostel.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike trails near Arguedas?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494774072/tour-of-bardenas-reales-by-mountain-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Bardenas Reales by mountain bike&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 80.7 km path that traverses the extensive semi-desert landscape, offering a significant test of endurance and skill.

One of the most impressive pieces of earth.

Another of the iconic "cabezos" of the Bardenas Reales park, which, as its name indicates, resembles the shape of an earthen castle that has still stood there for many centuries, guarding the wonderful landscape that surrounds it.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route typically takes around 1 hour and 23 minutes to complete. It's an easy ride suitable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an easy mountain bike ride and is suitable for all skill levels. It's a great option for beginners looking to experience the unique landscape of Bardenas Reales.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characteristic of a semi-desert landscape, featuring a mix of clay and sandstone. You can expect dramatic geological formations, barrancos (ravines), and potentially some sandy sections. The overall environment is arid but captivating.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers several striking highlights. You'll pass by the iconic natural monument of Castildetierra, a unique geological formation. You'll also encounter the Cabezo de las Cortinillas, which provides spectacular panoramic views, and the El Belcho Lagoons.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact trailhead isn't detailed, but generally, access to trails within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park involves designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local park regulations or komoot's tour details for the most precise starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Bardenas Reales for mountain biking is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are more moderate, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: While the Bardenas Reales Natural Park is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs on trails can vary. It's best to check the official park rules regarding pets, especially leash requirements, to ensure a compliant and enjoyable visit for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales, as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, generally does not require an entrance fee for visitors. However, certain activities or specific areas might have regulations or require permits. Always check the official park website for the latest information on access and rules before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with sections of other trails. Approximately 26% of the route follows the "Vuelta al Polígono de tiro" and about 2% overlaps with the "Barranco de Las Cortinas" trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the semi-desert environment, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling gear. A basic repair kit for your bike is also recommended. There are no water sources directly on the trail.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the Bardenas Reales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: While the park itself is quite remote, you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the nearby towns surrounding the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, such as Tudela or Arguedas. It's a good idea to plan your food and drink stops before or after your ride.
